target.title: fix(skills): prevent nested redirect when skills root already has skills
target.body:
## Summary
- **Problem:** When `~/.openclaw/skills/skills` subdirectory exists, the heuristic in `resolveNestedSkillsRoot` incorrectly redirected the skills root, causing skills in `~/.openclaw/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md` to be ignored.
- **Why it matters:** Users with a `skills/skills` subdirectory in their managed skills directory would lose access to all their installed skills without understanding why.
- **What changed:** Added early exit check in `resolveNestedSkillsRoot` to detect if `dir` is already a valid skills root (`dir/*/SKILL.md` exists, excluding `skills` subdirectory) before checking for nested redirect.
- **What did NOT change:** Repository-style skill trees (`repo/skills/*/SKILL.md`) continue to work correctly. The nested detection heuristic is still applied when appropriate.
## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra
## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra
## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression
## Root Cause (if applicable)
- **Root cause:** The `resolveNestedSkillsRoot` function applied a heuristic to all skill directories unconditionally: if `dir/skills` subdirectory exists and contains `SKILL.md`, redirect to that nested path. This was intended for repository-style skill trees but incorrectly triggered for managed skills directories.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No check to verify if the original directory is already a valid skills root before applying the nested redirect heuristic.
- **Contributing context:** The heuristic was designed for the pattern `repo/skills/*/SKILL.md` where `repo` is not a skills root but `repo/skills` is. It didn't account for cases where `dir` is already a valid root and `dir/skills` is just a coincidental subdirectory.
## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- **Coverage level that should have caught this:**
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts`
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** When a skills root directory has both `dir/my-skill/SKILL.md` and `dir/skills/decoy/SKILL.md`, the root-level skill `my-skill` should be loaded and `decoy` should NOT cause a redirect.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Unit test directly exercises `resolveNestedSkillsRoot` through `buildWorkspaceSkillSnapshot`, covering the exact code path that was buggy.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** None - this edge case was not covered.
## User-visible / Behavior Changes
- Skills in `~/.openclaw/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md` are now correctly loaded even when `~/.openclaw/skills/skills` subdirectory exists.
- No config changes, no migration needed.
## Diagram (if applicable)
```text
Before:
[~/.openclaw/skills/skills exists] -> [heuristic redirects to nested] -> [skills in root ignored]
After:
[~/.openclaw/skills/skills exists] -> [early exit: root has SKILL.md] -> [root skills loaded correctly]
Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
Repro + Verification
Environment
- OS: Linux/macOS/Windows
- Runtime/container: Node.js 22+
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: N/A
Steps
1. Create ~/.openclaw/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md with valid skill content
2. Create ~/.openclaw/skills/skills/decoy/SKILL.md with any content
3. Run openclaw skills check
Expected
- my-skill is listed as available skill
Actual (before fix)
- my-skill is not listed
- Only skills under skills/skills/ would be detected (if any)
Actual (after fix)
- my-skill is correctly listed
Evidence
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
  - Test added: src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts - "does not redirect to skills/skills when root already has skills"
Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios:
  - Ran pnpm test src/agents/skills.buildworkspaceskillsnapshot.test.ts - all 10 tests pass including new test case
  - Ran pnpm test src/agents/skills.test.ts - all 20 tests pass
  - Lint check passes
- Edge cases checked:
  - Skills root with fewer than 10 child directories
  - Skills root with skills subdirectory only (no other skills)
- What you did NOT verify:
  - Live testing with actual ~/.openclaw/skills/skills directory on production system
Review Conversations
- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: The early exit check (scanning up to 10 child directories) might miss skills roots with many non-skill directories before the first actual skill.
  - Mitigation: The limit of 10 is a reasonable heuristic - most skills roots will have skills in the first few entries. Users with unusual directory layouts can reorganize or use skills.load.extraDirs to specify roots explicitly.
- Risk: The check excludes only the skills subdirectory; other subdirectories named after skill concepts might still cause confusion.
  - Mitigation: This is acceptable because the heuristic is specifically about the dir/skills nested pattern, not general subdirectory filtering.
target.author: huangzynn

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary: The PR updates skills discovery to avoid redirecting to a nested `skills` directory when the configured root already contains a valid root-level skill, passes the skill file size cap into the probe, and adds a regression test for the root-plus-nested layout.
